chapters.title: Chapter 1 Classical Formation
chapters.description: In the classical formation after ...Nc6, d6, a6 and Nf6, Black threatens to halt White's momentum with ...Bg4. White can choose between the principled 8. Bf4 and the adventurous, Evans-style 8. b4.

chapters.title: Chapter 2 Scheveningen Formation
chapters.description: In Chapters 2 and 3, Black establishes the modest e6/d6 central pawn duo that resembles the Scheveningen structure, one of Black's most solid and flexible defensive systems in the Open Sicilian. White adopts the typical scheme: Qe2, Rd1 and Rac1.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 Scheveningen Formation II
chapters.description: In this second Scheveningen chapter, Black steps off the d-file with ...Qc7, with or without ...a6 inserted. White responds with Bf4, posting the bishop opposite Black's queen and putting pressure on d6.

chapters.title: Chapter 4 Siberian Variation
chapters.description: The Siberian Variation, where Black plays ...e6, Nc6 and Qc7, takes its name from the following trap: 8. Qe2?! Ng4 9. h3? Nd4. Instead, White should opt for 8. Nb5.

chapters.title: Chapter 5 Dragon Formation
chapters.description: Black intends to enter Dragon territory with kingside fianchetto. Standard development won't suffice for the gambiteer. White should either pressure f7 with Qb3 to provoke ...e6; or in other lines play 7. e5 to discourage ...d6.

chapters.title: Chapter 6 Paulsen Formation
chapters.description: In Chapters 6 and 7, Black builds on the Kan foundation of ...e6 and a6 before the paths diverge. With ...Nc6, the defender opts for the Paulsen and plans to refute the gambit via ...Nge7, Ng6, Be7 and O-O. The gambiteer halts Black's development with Bg5, provoking ...f6/h6.

chapters.title: Chapter 7 Kan Formation
chapters.description: In Chapters 6 and 7, Black builds on the Kan foundation of ...e6 and a6 before the paths diverge. Here, the defender postpones castling and instead expands on the queenside with ...b5 and Bb7. Against 8... b4, the knight advances with the thematic 9. Nd5, while 8... d6 is met by 9. Ng5, immediately targeting the weakened e6-square.

chapters.title: Chapter 8 Pin Defense
chapters.description: In this chapter, Black pins White's knight with ...Bb4. While the hasty 5...Bb4 is met by Qd4, the main line sees White respond with Qc2 and Rd1.

chapters.title: Chapter 9 Morphy Defense
chapters.description: Only after ...Bc5 does Black aim for ...Nge7 and O-O, rendering Bg5 less effective. White responds with e5, intending Ne4 to follow.

chapters.title: Chapter 10 Finegold Defense
chapters.description: In this chapter, Black defers ...Nc6, retaining the option of ...Nbd7. The main line continues 8... Be7 (the Finegold Defense), while 8... b5 leads to the Chicago Defense, typically followed by the ...Ra7 rook lift.

chapters.title: Chapter 11 Morra Declined Miscellany
chapters.description: This chapter covers Morra Declined structures and shows how White deals with miscellaneous Black responses.

chapters.title: Chapter 12 Push Variation
chapters.description: The "Maroczy Bind" is set up against 3...d3. White's move order matters: prophylactic h3 preventing ...Bg4/Ng4, and Nc3 after ...Nf6, avoiding ...Bxc3.
